heading: Special rules for section 1675(b) and 1675(c) reviews

children.heading: Determination of likelihood of continuation or recurrence of material injury
children.label: a Determination of likelihood of continuation or recurrence of material injury
A
children.text: its prior injury determinations, including the volume, price effect, and impact of imports of the subject merchandise on the industry before the order was issued or the suspension agreement was accepted,

children.label: B
children.text: whether any improvement in the state of the industry is related to the order or the suspension agreement,

children.label: C
children.text: whether the industry is vulnerable to material injury if the order is revoked or the suspension agreement is terminated, and

children.label: D
children.text: in an antidumping proceeding under , the findings of the administering authority regarding duty absorption under .section 1675(c) of this titlesection 1675(a)(4) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: any likely increase in production capacity or existing unused production capacity in the exporting country,

children.label: B
children.text: existing inventories of the subject merchandise, or likely increases in inventories,

children.label: C
children.text: the existence of barriers to the importation of such merchandise into countries other than the United States, and

children.label: D
children.text: the potential for product-shifting if production facilities in the foreign country, which can be used to produce the subject merchandise, are currently being used to produce other products.

children.label: A
children.text: there is likely to be significant price underselling by imports of the subject merchandise as compared to domestic like products, and

children.label: B
children.text: imports of the subject merchandise are likely to enter the United States at prices that otherwise would have a significant depressing or suppressing effect on the price of domestic like products.

children.label: A
children.text: likely declines in output, sales, market share, profits, productivity, return on investments, and utilization of capacity,

children.label: B
children.text: likely negative effects on cash flow, inventories, employment, wages, growth, ability to raise capital, and investment, and

children.label: C
children.text: likely negative effects on the existing development and production efforts of the industry, including efforts to develop a derivative or more advanced version of the domestic like product.

children.heading: Basis for determination
children.label: 5 Basis for determination
children.text: The presence or absence of any factor which the Commission is required to consider under this subsection shall not necessarily give decisive guidance with respect to the Commission’s determination of whether material injury is likely to continue or recur within a reasonably foreseeable time if the order is revoked or the suspended investigation is terminated. In making that determination, the Commission shall consider that the effects of revocation or termination may not be imminent, but may manifest themselves only over a longer period of time.

children.heading: Magnitude of margin of dumping and net countervailable subsidy; nature of countervailable subsidy
children.label: 6 Magnitude of margin of dumping and net countervailable subsidy; nature of countervailable subsidy
children.text: In making a determination under section 1675(b) or (c) of this title, the Commission may consider the magnitude of the margin of dumping or the magnitude of the net countervailable subsidy. If a countervailable subsidy is involved the Commission shall consider information regarding the nature of the countervailable subsidy and whether the subsidy is a subsidy described in Article 3 or 6.1 of the Subsidies Agreement.

children.heading: Cumulation
children.label: 7 Cumulation
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the Commission may cumulatively assess the volume and effect of imports of the subject merchandise from all countries with respect to which reviews under section 1675(b) or (c) of this title were initiated on the same day, if such imports would be likely to compete with each other and with domestic like products in the United States market. The Commission shall not cumulatively assess the volume and effects of imports of the subject merchandise in a case in which it determines that such imports are likely to have no discernible adverse impact on the domestic industry.

children.heading: Special rule for regional industries
children.label: 8 Special rule for regional industries
children.text: In a review under section 1675(b) or (c) of this title involving a regional industry, the Commission may base its determination on the regional industry defined in the original investigation under this subtitle, another region that satisfies the criteria established in , or the United States as a whole. In determining if a regional industry analysis is appropriate for the determination in the review, the Commission shall consider whether the criteria established in are likely to be satisfied if the order is revoked or the suspended investigation is terminated.section 1677(4)(C) of this titlesection 1677(4)(C) of this title

children.heading: Determination of likelihood of continuation or recurrence of a countervailable subsidy
children.label: b Determination of likelihood of continuation or recurrence of a countervailable subsidy
A
children.text: the net countervailable subsidy determined in the investigation and subsequent reviews, and

children.label: B
children.text: whether any change in the program which gave rise to the net countervailable subsidy described in subparagraph (A) has occurred that is likely to affect that net countervailable subsidy.

children.label: i
children.text: can potentially be used by the exporters or producers subject to the review under , andsection 1675(c) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: did not exist at the time that the countervailing duty order was issued or the suspension agreement was accepted, and

children.label: B
children.text: programs newly alleged to provide countervailable subsidies but only to the extent that the administering authority makes an affirmative countervailing duty determination with respect to such programs and with respect to the exporters or producers subject to the review.

children.heading: Net countervailable subsidy
children.label: 3 Net countervailable subsidy
children.text: The administering authority shall provide to the Commission the net countervailable subsidy that is likely to prevail if the order is revoked or the suspended investigation is terminated. The administering authority shall normally choose a net countervailable subsidy that was determined under or subsection (a) or (b)(1) of .section 1671d of this titlesection 1675 of this title

children.heading: Treatment of zero and de minimis rates
children.label: A Treatment of zero and de minimis rates
children.text: A net countervailable subsidy described in paragraph (1)(A) that is zero or de minimis shall not by itself require the administering authority to determine that revocation of a countervailing duty order or termination of a suspended investigation would not be likely to lead to continuation or recurrence of a countervailable subsidy.

children.heading: Application of de minimis standards
children.label: B Application of de minimis standards
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the administering authority shall apply the de minimis standards applicable to reviews conducted under subsections (a) and (b)(1) of .section 1675 of this title

children.heading: Determination of likelihood of continuation or recurrence of dumping
children.label: c Determination of likelihood of continuation or recurrence of dumping
A
children.text: the weighted average dumping margins determined in the investigation and subsequent reviews, and

children.label: B
children.text: the volume of imports of the subject merchandise for the period before and the period after the issuance of the antidumping duty order or acceptance of the suspension agreement.

children.heading: Consideration of other factors
children.label: 2 Consideration of other factors
children.text: If good cause is shown, the administering authority shall also consider such other price, cost, market, or economic factors as it deems relevant.

children.heading: Magnitude of the margin of dumping
children.label: 3 Magnitude of the margin of dumping
children.text: The administering authority shall provide to the Commission the magnitude of the margin of dumping that is likely to prevail if the order is revoked or the suspended investigation is terminated. The administering authority shall normally choose a margin that was determined under or under subsection (a) or (b)(1) of .section 1673d of this titlesection 1675 of this title

children.heading: Treatment of zero or de minimis margins
children.label: A Treatment of zero or de minimis margins
children.text: A dumping margin described in paragraph (1)(A) that is zero or de minimis shall not by itself require the administering authority to determine that revocation of an antidumping duty order or termination of a suspended investigation would not be likely to lead to continuation or recurrence of sales at less than fair value.

children.heading: Application of de minimis standards
children.label: B Application of de minimis standards
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the administering authority shall apply the de minimis standards applicable to reviews conducted under subsections (a) and (b) of .section 1675 of this title
